Fusion Core: The 50/50 Standard Utilities"Security is not a feature; it is a narrative of trust."Welcome to the Fusion Core library. This package provides the fundamental building blocks for creating Hybrid Cryptographic Applications that adhere to the Fusion 50/50 Standard.These utilities are designed to replace standard error handling and validation with Safe, Observable Primitives.🚀 Quick Start1. The Result Monad (Stop Throwing Exceptions)In high-assurance crypto, a decryption failure is a valid state, not a program crash. Use Result to handle it explicitly.import { Result, ok, err } from './result';

function decrypt(payload: string): Result<string, Error> {
  if (integrityCheck(payload)) {
    return ok("Secret Message");
  }
  return err(new Error("Integrity Violation")); // Returns value, doesn't throw
}

const outcome = decrypt(input);
if (outcome.isFailure()) {
  console.error(outcome.unwrapErr()); // Must handle error explicitly
}
2. Validating Hybrid KeysEnsure your application never accidentally relies on just one cryptographic primitive.import { Guard } from './guard';

const keyBundle = {
  classical: { /* ECC Key */ },
  quantum: { /* Kyber Key */ }
};

// Throws if either component is missing
if (Guard.isHybridKeyBundle(keyBundle)) {
  console.log("50/50 Compliance Verified");
}
📘 Explanations: The "Why"Why "Guard"?In a Hybrid Post-Quantum system, the most dangerous vulnerability is a downgrade attack. If an attacker can strip away the Quantum component of your key exchange and force your app to use only ECC (Classical), they can harvest your data today and decrypt it later with a Quantum Computer.Guard.isHybridKeyBundle is not just a type check; it is a policy enforcement. It ensures that the application refuses to operate unless both cryptographic engines are active.Why "Task"?We believe in Observability by Default. Standard Promises are opaque—you don't know how long they take or where they fail unless you wrap them in logs manually.Task wraps every operation in a Telemetry Span. When you run a Task, it automatically:Starts a timer.Generates a Trace ID.Records success/failure metrics.This is how we measure the "Quantum Delta" (the time difference between Classical and Quantum operations) without cluttering our business logic with timers.🛠 ReferencedeepMerge(target, source)Recursively merges objects.Security: Automatically strips __proto__, constructor, and prototype keys to prevent Prototype Pollution attacks.Use Case: Merging user-provided JSON configurations with secure defaults.Result<T, E>ok(value): Creates a success result.err(error): Creates a failure result.map(fn): Transforms the value inside ok, does nothing if err.unwrap(): Returns the value or throws if err.Guardrequired(val, name): Throws if null/undefined.byteLength(buffer, expected, name): Throws SecurityException if lengths don't match.isHybridKeyBundle(obj): Returns true only if object has classical and quantum properties.
